sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Nutrition support for adults: oral nutrition support, enteral tube feeding and parenteral nutrition — Recommendations
sources.version: NICE clinical guideline CG32, last updated 2017
sources.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE nutrition, dietetics, consent, capacity, and referral adaptation
sources.population: Adults in hospital and community settings with clinical concern for malnutrition or impaired intake.
sources.clinical_setting: General practice, community, hospital, nutrition, and multidisciplinary assessment.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for documenting unintentional weight loss, poor appetite, altered taste, impaired swallowing, altered bowel habit, reduced intake, BMI and percentage weight loss when measured. It does not calculate risk or generate nutrition support.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.6 — screening, clinical concern, weight loss, intake, and swallowing
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting unintentional weight loss, appetite, taste, swallowing, bowel habit, prolonged illness, BMI, percentage weight loss, reduced intake and future intake risk when actually assessed.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.3.1–1.3.4 — malnutrition and consent context
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-recorded malnutrition or risk criteria and consent or best-interest discussion; no diagnosis, calculation, feeding route, or treatment is generated.

archive.title: Nutrition support for adults: oral nutrition support, enteral tube feeding and parenteral nutrition — Recommendations
archive.publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence

archive.population: Adults in hospital and community settings with clinical concern for malnutrition or impaired intake.
archive.setting: General practice, community, hospital, nutrition, and multidisciplinary assessment.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.6 — screening, clinical concern, weight loss, intake, and swallowing
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting unintentional weight loss, appetite, taste, swallowing, bowel habit, prolonged illness, BMI, percentage weight loss, reduced intake and future intake risk when actually assessed.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.3.1–1.3.4 — malnutrition and consent context
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-recorded malnutrition or risk criteria and consent or best-interest discussion; no diagnosis, calculation, feeding route, or treatment is generated.

sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.population: Adults, children, and young people with overweight, obesity, or central adiposity, subject to section-specific age criteria.
sources.clinical_setting: Identification, sensitive discussion, assessment, behavioural intervention, referral, and follow-up.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for selected permission-sensitive adult weight discussion, measurements, context, previous attempts, goals, clinician-discussed intervention or referral, and follow-up fields. It does not support automatic advice, medicine selection, dosing, or UAE referral pathways.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.9.1–1.9.17 — permission, measurements, and adult assessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports avoiding diagnostic overshadowing, asking permission before weight discussion or measurements, and recording height, weight, BMI, waist or waist-to-height context and weight-related health risk.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.11.1–1.11.14 — results, context, intervention discussion, and referral
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports comorbidity, social or mental-health context, diet or activity goals, previous attempts, experiences, barriers, preferences, clinician-agreed intervention, and preference-sensitive referral documentation.

exact_sections.heading: Behavioural intervention recommendations — goals, progress, feedback, and follow-up
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting personalised goals, progress, barriers, support, and clinician-arranged review without inserting automatic lifestyle instructions.
